name: Cogent Biosciences, Inc.
business: Cogent Biosciences is a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing precision therapies for genetically defined diseases, with its most advanced program being bezuclastinib, a KIT inhibitor for Systemic Mastocytosis and gastrointestinal stromal tumors (GIST). The company is developing bezuclastinib for Non-Advanced Systemic Mastocytosis, Advanced Systemic Mastocytosis, and GIST, and expects to launch commercially in the United States in the second half of 2026 pending regulatory approval.

exposures.sentence: Our most advanced program is bezuclastinib, also known as CGT9486, a highly selective tyrosine kinase inhibitor that is designed to potently inhibit the KIT D816V mutation as well as other mutations in KIT exon 17.
We also have an ongoing Phase 1 study of our novel internally developed FGFR2/3 inhibitor and have initiated a Phase 1 study of our CNS-penetrant, selective mutant ErbB2 inhibitor.
In addition, the Cogent Research Team is developing a portfolio of novel targeted therapies to help patients fighting serious, genetically driven diseases targeting mutations in PI3K , KRAS and JAK2.

catalysts.event: Submit NDA for bezuclastinib in patients with AdvSM
catalysts.sentence: We submitted the first New Drug Application ( NDA ) for bezuclastinib in patients with NonAdvSM in December 2025 and expect to submit an NDA in the first half of 2026 for patients with AdvSM.
We expect to complete the GIST NDA submission in April 2026.

catalysts.event: Initiate Phase 2 trial for bezuclastinib combination in first-line GIST
catalysts.sentence: In mid-2026, we expect to initiate a Phase 2 trial investigating the benefit of the bezuclastinib combination for first-line GIST patients with exon 9 mutations who are naive to, or recently initiated treatment with, imatinib.

catalysts.event: Initiate Phase 1 dose escalation for CGT6297
catalysts.sentence: We expect to initiate a Phase 1 dose escalation in the first quarter of 2026.
We plan to submit an IND for this program in 2026.
We plan to submit an IND for this program in 2026.

catalysts.event: Commercial launch of bezuclastinib in the United States for SM and GIST
catalysts.sentence: Pending regulatory approval, we expect to launch bezuclastinib commercially in the United States in the second half of 2026 to treat patients with NonAdvSM.

catalysts.event: Share clinical data from Phase 1 study of CGT4859
catalysts.sentence: We expect to share clinical data from our Phase 1 study in 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Global annual market opportunity of over $4 billion for bezuclastinib in combination with sunitinib as a potential second-line treatment for patients with GIST.
tamClaims.sentence: Based on internal analyses and external market research, we estimate a global annual market opportunity of over $4 billion for bezuclastinib in combination with sunitinib as a potential second-line treatment for patients with GIST, approximately $3.5 billion for the treatment of patients with NonAdvSM, and approximately $500 million for the treatment of patients with AdvSM.

tamClaims.claim: Global annual market opportunity of approximately $3.5 billion for the treatment of patients with NonAdvSM.
tamClaims.sentence: Based on internal analyses and external market research, we estimate a global annual market opportunity of over $4 billion for bezuclastinib in combination with sunitinib as a potential second-line treatment for patients with GIST, approximately $3.5 billion for the treatment of patients with NonAdvSM, and approximately $500 million for the treatment of patients with AdvSM.
